message: Environment file committed to version control. This file typically contains secret API keys, database credentials, and session salts.
fix: Remove the file from git history (`git rm --cached .env`), add it to your `.gitignore`, and rotate all credentials contained within it immediately.

message: Firebase or Google Cloud Service Account Key file committed. Anyone who has this key has admin privileges to your Firestore database and Cloud services.
fix: Revoke the service account key in the Google Cloud Console immediately. Delete the file from the repository, and load service account details using environment variables.

message: SSH Private Key committed. Attackers can use this key to authenticate to servers or remote machines.
fix: Remove the key from git history, replace the key pair in all target servers/accounts, and add `id_*` keys to `.gitignore`.

message: Authentication config file containing system or registry access tokens committed.
fix: Remove the file, revoke the corresponding tokens (npm token, PyPI token, etc.), and use environment variable injection for CI/CD authentication.

message: SQLite database committed. This is risky because it exposes internal data structure and schema, and can leak user data or metadata from testing.
fix: Remove the SQLite database file from git history. Configure your application to create a new database on start, or use a hosted database server. Add database files to `.gitignore`.

message: Private Certificate or Key Store file committed. Exposes SSL/TLS private keys or application signing credentials.
fix: Revoke the certificate and key, issue new certificates, and avoid storing certificates directly in the codebase. Use secrets managers.
